documentation.value: Returns markup that is not HTML-encoded. Use with caution to avoid XSS vulnerabilities.
The HTML string to render without encoding.

signatures.label: @Html.ActionLink(linkText: string, actionName: string, controllerName: string, routeValues: object, htmlAttributes: object): IHtmlContent

documentation.value: Generates an HTML anchor element (<a>) for the specified controller action.
The text to display for the link.
The name of the action method.
The name of the controller.
An anonymous object containing route values (e.g., new { id = 5 }).
An anonymous object containing HTML attributes (e.g., new { @class = "btn" }).
Renders a partial view as an HTML string. Prefer @await Html.PartialAsync() for async rendering.
The name of the partial view to render (e.g., "_LoginPartial").
Optional model object to pass to the partial view.
Generates an HTML text input element for the specified model property with model binding.
A lambda expression identifying the model property (e.g., m => m.Name).
An anonymous object containing HTML attributes.
Accesses a property on the view's model. The model type is defined by the @model directive at the top of the view.
The name of the property to access on the model.
Accesses dynamic data passed from the controller to the view via the ViewBag dictionary.
The dynamic property name set in the controller.
Defines a named section in a view that can be rendered in the layout using @RenderSection.
The name of the section (e.g., Scripts, Styles).
The HTML and Razor content for this section.
Defines C# methods and properties within a Razor view. In Razor Pages, use @functions for page-level logic.
C# methods, properties, and fields available within the view.
Imports a C# namespace into the Razor view, making its types available without full qualification.
The fully qualified namespace to import (e.g., System.Linq, MyApp.Models).
Injects a service from the dependency injection container into the Razor view.
The type of the service to inject (e.g., IConfiguration, ILogger<IndexModel>).
The property name to access the service within the view.
